title: HB 1521 — Restroom restriction (government buildings)
description: Prohibits use of government building restrooms that don't match biological sex; criminal misdemeanor for violations.
long_description:
Florida HB 1521, signed May 17, 2023, requires all persons to use restrooms in government-owned buildings that correspond to their biological sex. Violations are a second-degree misdemeanor (up to 60 days jail / $500 fine). Applies to schools, state offices, prisons, and other government facilities. A transgender person who doesn't immediately leave a restroom after being asked may be charged.

**Source:** [MAP — Bathroom/Facility Policies](https://www.lgbtmap.org/equality-maps/non_discrimination/bathroom_safe_haven_laws) · [Bill text](https://www.flsenate.gov/Session/Bill/2023/1521)

title: SB 458 — Restroom restriction (public facilities)
description: Restricts use of public facility restrooms by biological sex; misdemeanor for non-compliance.
long_description:
Montana SB 458, signed April 28, 2023, requires biological sex-based use of restrooms and changing facilities in public buildings. Violations are a misdemeanor. Applies to government offices, schools, and other public facilities.

**Source:** [MAP — Bathroom/Facility Policies](https://www.lgbtmap.org/equality-maps/non_discrimination/bathroom_safe_haven_laws)

title: HB 421 — Restroom restriction (public schools and agencies)
description: Restricts restroom use in public schools and state agencies by biological sex.
long_description:
Idaho HB 421, signed April 4, 2023, limits restroom and changing facility use in public schools and state agencies to biological sex. Violations are a misdemeanor.

**Source:** [MAP — Bathroom/Facility Policies](https://www.lgbtmap.org/equality-maps/non_discrimination/bathroom_safe_haven_laws)

title: HB 25 — School restroom restriction
description: Restricts restroom and locker room use in public schools to biological sex; policy enforcement, no criminal penalty.
long_description:
Texas HB 25, signed October 25, 2021, requires public K–12 students and UIL (University Interscholastic League) athletes to use restrooms and changing facilities that match their biological sex. Enforcement is through school policy compliance and UIL eligibility rules — there is no standalone criminal penalty for students. The law also prohibits transgender girls from competing on female sports teams.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2021/TX/HB25)

title: SF 496 — School restroom restriction and education provisions
description: Restricts K–12 school restroom use by biological sex; bans discussion of gender identity in early grades.
long_description:
Iowa SF 496, signed May 26, 2023, is a comprehensive K–12 education law that includes a requirement for students to use restrooms and changing facilities matching their biological sex. It also prohibits instruction on gender identity in grades K–6 and bars school staff from using a student's preferred pronouns without parental consent. Enforcement is through school policy; there is no standalone criminal penalty for students.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/IA/SF496)

title: HB 470 — School restroom restriction (Kentucky)
description: Restricts restroom and locker room use in public schools to biological sex.
long_description:
Kentucky HB 470, signed March 24, 2023, is an omnibus anti-trans bill that includes a requirement for K–12 students to use restrooms matching their biological sex. Enforcement is through school policy. The same bill also bans gender-affirming healthcare for minors and restricts trans girls from female sports teams. See KY-HB470-2023 for the healthcare provisions.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/KY/HB470)

title: SB 32 — SAFE Act (gender-affirming care ban for minors)
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for transgender minors; first such law in the nation. Felony for healthcare providers.
long_description:
Arkansas SB 32, the Save Adolescents from Experimentation (SAFE) Act, became law April 6, 2021 after the legislature overrode Governor Hutchinson's veto — the first state ban on gender-affirming care for minors in US history. Healthcare providers who prescribe puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors face a Class D felony. The law was blocked by courts for two years but took effect in 2023 after the Eighth Circuit lifted an injunction.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2021/AR/SB32) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 184 — Vulnerable Child Compassion and Protection Act
description: Bans gender-affirming care for minors; felony charge for healthcare providers.
long_description:
Alabama SB 184, the Vulnerable Child Compassion and Protection Act, signed April 8, 2022 by Governor Kay Ivey, makes it a Class C felony for healthcare providers to prescribe puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to transgender minors under 19. Also prohibits school staff from referring students to gender-affirming care without parental consent. The law was partially blocked by courts but the felony healthcare provision was upheld.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2022/AL/SB184) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 1040 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Prohibits healthcare providers from performing gender-affirming procedures or prescribing hormones to minors.
long_description:
Arizona SB 1040, signed April 14, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from administering puberty blockers, cross-sex hormones, or performing gender-affirming surgeries on minors. Violations can result in license revocation. The law includes a grandfather clause for minors already receiving treatment.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/AZ/SB1040) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 254 — Restricting gender-affirming care (minors and adults)
description: Bans gender-affirming care for minors and restricts it for adults; active enforcement by the Board of Medicine.
long_description:
Florida SB 254, signed May 17, 2023, bans gender-affirming medical care for minors and restricts access for adults by requiring in-person visits with a physician (ending telehealth-based prescriptions). The Florida Board of Medicine and Board of Osteopathic Medicine have actively enforced these rules, suspending licenses of providers who continue treating trans patients. This is among the most actively enforced healthcare bans in the country.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/FL/SB254) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 140 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Prohibits gender-affirming medical care for transgender minors; license revocation for violations.
long_description:
Georgia SB 140, signed March 23, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from administering puberty blockers, cross-sex hormones, or performing gender-affirming surgeries on patients under 18. Violations can result in loss of medical license and civil liability. Minors already receiving treatment had until July 1, 2023 to discontinue.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/GA/SB140) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 71 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors (felony)
description: Bans gender-affirming care for minors; felony for healthcare providers.
long_description:
Idaho HB 71, signed April 4, 2023, makes it a felony for healthcare providers to administer puberty blockers, cross-sex hormones, or gender-affirming surgeries to minors. Providers face up to 10 years in prison. Idaho is one of only a few states with a felony (not just license revocation) as the primary penalty for healthcare providers.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/ID/HB71) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 480 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for healthcare providers.
long_description:
Indiana SB 480, signed April 5, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation and potential civil liability. Minors receiving treatment were required to wind down care.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/IN/SB480) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SF 482 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for transgender minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Iowa SF 482, signed May 26, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers, cross-sex hormones, or performing gender-affirming surgeries on patients under 18. Violations can result in license revocation. Note: verify bill number against Trans Legislation Tracker — Iowa passed multiple anti-trans bills on the same day in 2023.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/IA/SF482) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 470 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors (Kentucky)
description: Omnibus bill banning gender-affirming care for minors; also restricts school restrooms and sports.
long_description:
Kentucky HB 470, signed March 24, 2023, is an omnibus anti-trans bill that bans gender-affirming medical care for minors (puberty blockers, hormones, surgeries) with license revocation as the primary penalty for providers. The same bill also restricts school restroom use to biological sex and bars transgender girls from female sports teams. One of the most comprehensive single-bill anti-trans laws passed in 2023.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/KY/HB470) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 9 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for transgender minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Louisiana SB 9, signed June 16, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. Louisiana also enacted separate legislation restricting school bathroom use and prohibiting classroom discussion of gender identity.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/LA/SB9) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 2753 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Mississippi SB 2753, signed February 28, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to patients under 18, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. Mississippi was among the earliest states to enact a healthcare ban in the 2023 legislative wave.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/MS/SB2753) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 49 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Missouri SB 49, signed June 7, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to patients under 18, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in loss of medical license. Missouri's Attorney General also issued an emergency rule in 2023 attempting to restrict access for adults, but it was not sustained through the courts.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/MO/SB49) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 99 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Montana SB 99, signed April 28, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to patients under 18, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation and civil liability. Montana passed this healthcare ban on the same day as its bathroom restriction law (SB 458).

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/MT/SB99) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: LB 574 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Nebraska LB 574, signed May 22, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to patients under 19, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. The bill also bans abortion after 12 weeks; the two provisions were combined to secure enough votes for passage.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/NE/LB574) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 49 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
North Carolina SB 49, signed August 16, 2023 by Governor Roy Cooper (a Democrat), passed with bipartisan support including several Democratic legislators. The bill bans gender-affirming care for minors with license revocation as the primary penalty for providers. Cooper stated he signed it to avoid a veto override that would have included more restrictive school provisions.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/NC/SB49) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 1254 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
North Dakota HB 1254, signed April 24, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation and civil liability. North Dakota passed several anti-trans bills during the same 2023 session.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/ND/HB1254) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 68 — CARE Act (gender-affirming care ban for minors)
description: Bans gender-affirming care for minors; enacted via veto override.
long_description:
Ohio HB 68, the Condemning Adolescent Radical Experimentation (CARE) Act, became law January 4, 2024 when the legislature overrode Governor DeWine's December 2023 veto. The law bans gender-affirming medical care for minors and prohibits transgender girls from competing on female school sports teams. It is one of the few healthcare bans enacted via legislative override of a Republican governor.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2024/OH/HB68) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 1011 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Oklahoma HB 1011, signed May 3, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. Oklahoma passed this as part of a broader package of anti-trans legislation during the 2023 session.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/OK/HB1011) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 1080 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
South Dakota HB 1080, signed February 13, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. South Dakota was among the first states to enact a healthcare ban in the 2023 legislative session.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/SD/HB1080) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 1 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Tennessee SB 1, signed March 22, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to patients under 18, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. Tennessee was the first state in the 2023 wave to enact a healthcare ban, and also passed a separate drag performance restriction (SB 3) in the same session.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/TN/SB1) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 14 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; enforced via AG investigations and license revocation.
long_description:
Texas SB 14, signed June 2, 2023, bans gender-affirming medical care for minors and requires transgender minors already receiving care to wind down treatment. Enforcement is through the Medical Board (license revocation) and the Texas Attorney General, who has actively investigated providers and hospitals. Texas's AG was already investigating families under a prior executive order before this law passed, making Texas one of the most aggressively enforced states.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/TX/SB14) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 16 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Utah SB 16, signed January 28, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation. Utah was among the first states to enact a healthcare ban in 2023 — earlier than most of the legislative wave that followed.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/UT/SB16) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: HB 2007 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
West Virginia HB 2007, signed March 11, 2023, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to patients under 18, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation and civil liability.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/WV/HB2007) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SF 133 — Gender-affirming care ban for minors
description: Bans gender-affirming medical care for minors; license revocation for providers.
long_description:
Wyoming SF 133, signed March 21, 2024, prohibits healthcare providers from prescribing puberty blockers or cross-sex hormones to minors, or performing gender-affirming surgeries. Violations result in license revocation and civil liability. Wyoming joined the majority of Republican-led states in banning gender-affirming care for minors.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2024/WY/SF133) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/healthcare/youth_medical_care_bans_for_trans_people)

title: SB 1438 — Protection of Children Act (drag performance restriction)
description: Restricts adult live performances in venues where minors may be present; civil fines and license revocation.
long_description:
Florida SB 1438, signed May 26, 2023, prohibits venues from admitting minors to 'adult live performances' — language broadly interpreted to include drag shows. Venues that violate the law risk losing their liquor license and face civil fines. The law has been challenged in federal court; a preliminary injunction was issued in 2023 but the law has been partially enforced pending appeals.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/FL/SB1438)

title: SB 3 — Adult Entertainment Act (drag performance restriction)
description: Restricts 'adult cabaret entertainment' including drag performances in public or where minors may be present.
long_description:
Tennessee SB 3, the Adult Entertainment Act, signed March 2, 2023, restricts 'adult cabaret entertainment' — defined to include male or female impersonators — in public spaces or anywhere minors might be present. It is a Class A misdemeanor for a first offense and a Class E felony for repeat violations. A federal court initially struck the law down as unconstitutional, but the Sixth Circuit narrowed the ruling; enforcement in practice has been limited.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/TN/SB3)

title: SB 107 — Transgender Refuge Act (shield law)
description: Prohibits California agencies from cooperating with other states' investigations related to gender-affirming care.
long_description:
California SB 107, the Transgender Refuge Act, signed September 29, 2022 by Governor Newsom, prohibits California courts and agencies from cooperating with other states' civil or criminal proceedings related to gender-affirming care provided in California. Protects healthcare providers who treat out-of-state patients. Combined with California's existing comprehensive non-discrimination protections across employment, housing, and public accommodations, California is one of the strongest sanctuary states in the nation.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2022/CA/SB107) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/non_discrimination_laws/public-accommodations)

title: SF 2909 — Minnesota Leads Act (sanctuary + shield law)
description: Establishes Minnesota as a sanctuary state for transgender individuals and their families; shield provisions against other states' enforcement.
long_description:
Minnesota SF 2909, the Minnesota Leads Act, signed May 17, 2023 by Governor Tim Walz, establishes Minnesota as a sanctuary state for transgender individuals. It prohibits Minnesota courts and agencies from cooperating with other states' investigations or proceedings related to gender-affirming care, and provides legal protection for individuals who travel to Minnesota for care. The law also strengthens Minnesota's existing non-discrimination protections. Minnesota is considered one of the most affirming states in the Midwest.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/MN/SF2909) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/non_discrimination_laws/public-accommodations)

title: SB 5599 — Sanctuary for minors seeking gender-affirming care
description: Allows shelters to house transgender minors without parental notification when seeking gender-affirming care; limits extradition cooperation.
long_description:
Washington SB 5599, signed April 27, 2023 by Governor Jay Inslee, allows youth shelters and host home programs to house transgender minors without notifying parents in certain cases, particularly when the minor is seeking gender-affirming care or fleeing abuse. It also limits Washington's cooperation with other states seeking to extradite individuals related to gender-affirming care. Washington already has some of the strongest transgender non-discrimination protections in the country across employment, housing, and public accommodations.

**Source:** [Trans Legislation Tracker](https://translegislation.com/bills/2023/WA/SB5599) · [MAP](https://www.mapresearch.org/equality-maps/non_discrimination_laws/public-accommodations)
